name: Laura Lee
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary instrumentation • Spectroscopy and telescopic observations • Astrobiology
bio: Laura Lee is a PhD candidate in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University working in the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). Her research focuses on Preparing Future Planetary Missions through her role as the Instrument Scientist for the VISIONS cameras on NASAs ESCAPADE mission to Mars, collecting ground-based telescopic observations to classify targets in the Emirates Mission to the Asteroid Belt, and leading extraterrestrial horticulture studies on crop growth responses to fungi in lunar and martian analog soils. Lee's work combines instrumentation, spectroscopy, and astrobiology through multidisciplinary collaborations to help advance the future of space exploration.
